dataset: CLUTRR (Compositional Language Understanding with Text-based Relational Reasoning)
purpose: Source for the nsk1 flagship 'CLUTRR-derived covered slice' eval (k=2 up-edge grandparent items).
epistemic_status: MEASURED — every count/column below is read directly from the fetched artifacts by the command in `verification.command`; no interpretation of any experimental outcome is recorded here (provenance only).

license.name: Creative Commons Attribution-NonCommercial 4.0 International
license.note: Non-commercial use only. Attribution: Sinha et al., EMNLP 2019 (arXiv:1908.06177). Raw data + generator clone are gitignored (see .gitignore); this manifest + fetch.sh are the committed, sha-pinned re-fetch record — mirroring data/physics-qudt/ (source gitignored, manifest committed).

citation: Koustuv Sinha, Shagun Sodhani, Jin Dong, Joelle Pineau, William L. Hamilton. CLUTRR: A Diagnostic Benchmark for Inductive Reasoning from Text. EMNLP 2019. arXiv:1908.06177.

generator_repo.note: Generator only (produces CSVs from a MongoDB backend; running it end-to-end requires pymongo + a MongoDB instance). The relation vocabulary lives in clutrr/store/relations_store.yaml; the composition rules in clutrr/store/rules_store.yaml.

released_data.source: Google Drive file id 1SEq_e1IVCDDzsBIBhoUQ5pOVH5kxRoZF (linked from the generator README; the pre-generated datasets used in the EMNLP-2019 paper).

task_naming: task_id.relation_length, e.g. '1.2' = Task 1 (basic family relations, free of noise), relation length k=2. Task 1 is the clean, noise-free task.

structured_columns_explained.story_edges: list of directed node-index edges forming the reasoning chain, e.g. [(0, 1), (1, 2)].
structured_columns_explained.edge_types: surface relation per story edge, in story order, e.g. ['father', 'father']. f_comb is these hyphen-joined.
structured_columns_explained.f_comb: hyphen-joined edge_types, e.g. 'father-father'. In the generator: relation_comb = '-'.join(edge rel for edge in story) (clutrr/relations/puzzle.py). For k=2 up-edge grandparent items f_comb in {father-father, father-mother, mother-father, mother-mother}.
structured_columns_explained.proof_state: resolution chain: target triple -> list of atomic supporting triples, e.g. [{('Jennifer','grandfather','Jason'): [('Jennifer','father','Michael'),('Michael','father','Jason')]}].
structured_columns_explained.target: gold answer relation (the query-edge relation), e.g. 'grandfather'.
structured_columns_explained.query_edge: node-index pair being queried, e.g. (0, 2); 'query' is the entity-name pair, e.g. ('Jennifer','Jason').
structured_columns_explained.clean_story: natural-language story with no noise (task 1). NOTE: surface phrasing of an up-edge may render via the child relation ('Michael and his daughter Jennifer') even where edge_types/f_comb is the canonical up-edge ('father'); the canonical up-edge in edge_types/proof_state is what defines the slice.

relation_vocabulary.source: clutrr/store/relations_store.yaml (rel field per relation-key x gender)
relation_vocabulary.composition_rule_for_slice: rules_store.yaml compositional.family: (inv-child, inv-child) -> inv-grand, i.e. parent-of-parent = grandparent. This is exactly the k=2 up-edge grandparent case.

k2_up_edge_grandparent_slice.definition: k=2 items whose f_comb is up-edges only over {mother,father} (f_comb in {father-father, father-mother, mother-father, mother-mother}) with target in {grandmother, grandfather}.
k2_up_edge_grandparent_slice.NOT_in: data_089907f8/'1.2_test.csv' (38 rows) — this bundle is a holdout/inductive split ('Generalization - holdout - clean'), so its k=2 TEST patterns are only {grandson-brother, husband-daughter}; the up-up grandparent patterns live in the TRAIN csv. A covered slice of clean k=2 up-edge grandparent items should therefore be drawn from '1.2,1.3_train.csv' (or generated fresh), not from '1.2_test.csv'.

example_row.clean_story: [Michael] and his daughter [Jennifer] like to read poems together. [Jason] is the proud father of the lovely [Michael]

verification.command: python3 (see data/clutrr-cache/fetch.sh --verify) reads clutrr-emnlp-release.zip -> data_089907f8.zip -> the two k=2 csvs and tabulates f_comb/target for rows with 2 atomic relations.
